segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why was the first tunnel not completed?

translations.zh-Hans: 第一条隧道为什么没有建成？

segments.text: In 1858, a French engineer, Aimé Thomé de Gamond, arrived in England with a plan for a 21-mile tunnel under the English Channel.

translations.zh-Hans: 1858年，法国工程师艾梅·托梅·德·加蒙带着一项在英吉利海峡下修建21英里隧道的计划来到英国。

segments.text: He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the center of the channel.

translations.zh-Hans: 他说，可以在海峡中央修建一个平台。

segments.text: This platform would serve as a port and a railway station.

translations.zh-Hans: 这个平台可兼作港口和火车站。

segments.text: The tunnel would be well ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果在海平面以上建造高烟囱，隧道就能保持良好通风。

segments.text: In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Lowe.

translations.zh-Hans: 1860年，英国人威廉·洛提出了一项更好的计划。

segments.text: He suggested that a double railway tunnel should be built.

translations.zh-Hans: 他建议修建一条双轨铁路隧道。

segments.text: This would solve the problem of ventilation, for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it.

translations.zh-Hans: 这样可以解决通风问题，因为火车进入这条隧道时，会把新鲜空气吸到后方。

segments.text: 42 years later a tunnel was actually begun.

translations.zh-Hans: 42年后，一条隧道真正开始动工。

segments.text: If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果当时英国人不害怕入侵，隧道本来会建成。

segments.text: The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel.

translations.zh-Hans: 世界又等了将近100年，才迎来英吉利海峡隧道。

segments.text: It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent.

translations.zh-Hans: 隧道于1994年3月7日正式开通，终于把英国和欧洲大陆连接起来。
